title: lantana viburnoides subsp. masaica
intro: Lantana viburnoides subsp. masaica is a plant species within the Lantana genus, characterized by its shrub-like growth and clusters of small, fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Lantana viburnoides subsp. masaica include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are commonly used in folk medicine to promote overall well-being and prevent illnesses.
